members.name: GetChannels
members.summary: Returns the number of channels allocated for communication with peer.

members.name: GetPacketFlags
members.summary: Returns the ENet flags of the next packet in the received queue. See [code]FLAG_*[/code] constants for available packet flags. Note that not all flags are replicated from the sending peer to the receiving peer.

members.name: GetRemoteAddress
members.summary: Returns the IP address of this peer.

members.name: GetRemotePort
members.summary: Returns the remote port of this peer.

members.name: GetStatistic
members.summary: Returns the requested [param statistic] for this peer.

members.name: IsActive
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the peer is currently active (i.e. the associated [ENetConnection] is still valid).

members.name: PeerDisconnect
members.summary: Request a disconnection from a peer. An [constant ENetConnection.EVENT_DISCONNECT] will be generated during [method ENetConnection.service] once the disconnection is complete.

members.name: PeerDisconnectLater
members.summary: Request a disconnection from a peer, but only after all queued outgoing packets are sent. An [constant ENetConnection.EVENT_DISCONNECT] will be generated during [method ENetConnection.service] once the disconnection is complete.

members.name: PeerDisconnectNow
members.summary: Force an immediate disconnection from a peer. No [constant ENetConnection.EVENT_DISCONNECT] will be generated. The foreign peer is not guaranteed to receive the disconnect notification, and is reset immediately upon return from this function.

members.name: Ping
members.summary: Sends a ping request to a peer. ENet automatically pings all connected peers at regular intervals, however, this function may be called to ensure more frequent ping requests.

members.name: PingInterval
members.summary: Sets the [param ping_interval] in milliseconds at which pings will be sent to a peer. Pings are used both to monitor the liveness of the connection and also to dynamically adjust the throttle during periods of low traffic so that the throttle has reasonable responsiveness during traffic spikes. The default ping interval is [code]500[/code] milliseconds.

members.name: Reset
members.summary: Forcefully disconnects a peer. The foreign host represented by the peer is not notified of the disconnection and will timeout on its connection to the local host.

members.name: Send
members.signature: public int Send(int channel, PackedByteArray packet, int flags)
members.summary: Queues a [param packet] to be sent over the specified [param channel]. See [code]FLAG_*[/code] constants for available packet flags.

members.name: SetTimeout
members.signature: public void SetTimeout(int timeout, int timeoutMin, int timeoutMax)
members.summary: Sets the timeout parameters for a peer. The timeout parameters control how and when a peer will timeout from a failure to acknowledge reliable traffic. Timeout values are expressed in milliseconds. The [param timeout] is a factor that, multiplied by a value based on the average round trip time, will determine the timeout limit for a reliable packet. When that limit is reached, the timeout will be doubled, and the peer will be disconnected if that limit has reached [param timeout_min]. The [param timeout_max] parameter, on the other hand, defines a fixed timeout for which any packet must be acknowledged or the peer will be dropped.

members.name: ThrottleConfigure
members.signature: public void ThrottleConfigure(int interval, int acceleration, int deceleration)
members.summary: Configures throttle parameter for a peer. Unreliable packets are dropped by ENet in response to the varying conditions of the Internet connection to the peer. The throttle represents a probability that an unreliable packet should not be dropped and thus sent by ENet to the peer. By measuring fluctuations in round trip times of reliable packets over the specified [param interval], ENet will either increase the probability by the amount specified in the [param acceleration] parameter, or decrease it by the amount specified in the [param deceleration] parameter (both are ratios to [constant PACKET_THROTTLE_SCALE]). When the throttle has a value of [constant PACKET_THROTTLE_SCALE], no unreliable packets are dropped by ENet, and so 100% of all unreliable packets will be sent. When the throttle has a value of [code]0[/code], all unreliable packets are dropped by ENet, and so 0% of all unreliable packets will be sent. Intermediate values for the throttle represent intermediate probabilities between 0% and 100% of unreliable packets being sent. The bandwidth limits of the local and foreign hosts are taken into account to determine a sensible limit for the throttle probability above which it should not raise even in the best of conditions.

values.name: STATE_ACKNOWLEDGING_CONNECT
values.summary: The peer has acknowledged the connection request.

values.name: STATE_ACKNOWLEDGING_DISCONNECT
values.summary: The peer has acknowledged the disconnection request.

values.name: STATE_CONNECTED
values.summary: The peer is currently connected and ready to communicate with.

values.name: STATE_CONNECTING
values.summary: The peer is currently attempting to connect.

values.name: STATE_CONNECTION_PENDING
values.summary: The peer is currently connecting.

values.name: STATE_CONNECTION_SUCCEEDED
values.summary: The peer has successfully connected, but is not ready to communicate with yet ([constant STATE_CONNECTED]).

values.name: STATE_DISCONNECTED
values.summary: The peer is disconnected.

values.name: STATE_DISCONNECTING
values.summary: The peer is currently disconnecting.

values.name: STATE_DISCONNECT_LATER
values.summary: The peer is expected to disconnect after it has no more outgoing packets to send.

values.name: STATE_ZOMBIE
values.summary: The peer has lost connection, but is not considered truly disconnected (as the peer didn't acknowledge the disconnection request).

values.name: PEER_LAST_ROUND_TRIP_TIME
values.summary: Last recorded round trip time for a reliable packet.

values.name: PEER_LAST_ROUND_TRIP_TIME_VARIANCE
values.summary: Variance of the last trip time recorded.

values.name: PEER_PACKET_LOSS
values.summary: Mean packet loss of reliable packets as a ratio with respect to the [constant PACKET_LOSS_SCALE].

values.name: PEER_PACKET_LOSS_EPOCH
values.summary: The time at which packet loss statistics were last updated (in milliseconds since the connection started). The interval for packet loss statistics updates is 10 seconds, and at least one packet must have been sent since the last statistics update.

values.name: PEER_PACKET_LOSS_VARIANCE
values.summary: Packet loss variance.

values.name: PEER_PACKET_THROTTLE
values.summary: The peer's current throttle status.

values.name: PEER_PACKET_THROTTLE_ACCELERATION
values.summary: The throttle's acceleration factor. Higher values will make ENet adapt to fluctuating network conditions faster, causing unrelaible packets to be sent [i]more[/i] often. The default value is [code]2[/code].

values.name: PEER_PACKET_THROTTLE_COUNTER
values.summary: Internal value used to increment the packet throttle counter. The value is hardcoded to [code]7[/code] and cannot be changed. You probably want to look at [constant PEER_PACKET_THROTTLE_ACCELERATION] instead.

values.name: PEER_PACKET_THROTTLE_DECELERATION
values.summary: The throttle's deceleration factor. Higher values will make ENet adapt to fluctuating network conditions faster, causing unrelaible packets to be sent [i]less[/i] often. The default value is [code]2[/code].

values.name: PEER_PACKET_THROTTLE_EPOCH
values.summary: The time at which throttle statistics were last updated (in milliseconds since the connection started). The interval for throttle statistics updates is [constant PEER_PACKET_THROTTLE_INTERVAL].

values.name: PEER_PACKET_THROTTLE_INTERVAL
values.summary: The interval over which the lowest mean round trip time should be measured for use by the throttle mechanism (in milliseconds). The default value is [code]5000[/code].

values.name: PEER_PACKET_THROTTLE_LIMIT
values.summary: The maximum number of unreliable packets that should not be dropped. This value is always greater than or equal to [code]1[/code]. The initial value is equal to [constant PACKET_THROTTLE_SCALE].

values.name: PEER_ROUND_TRIP_TIME
values.summary: Mean packet round trip time for reliable packets.

values.name: PEER_ROUND_TRIP_TIME_VARIANCE
values.summary: Variance of the mean round trip time.

constants.name: STATE_DISCONNECTED
constants.summary: The peer is disconnected.

constants.name: STATE_CONNECTING
constants.summary: The peer is currently attempting to connect.

constants.name: STATE_ACKNOWLEDGING_CONNECT
constants.summary: The peer has acknowledged the connection request.

constants.name: STATE_CONNECTION_PENDING
constants.summary: The peer is currently connecting.

constants.name: STATE_CONNECTION_SUCCEEDED
constants.summary: The peer has successfully connected, but is not ready to communicate with yet ([constant STATE_CONNECTED]).

constants.name: STATE_CONNECTED
constants.summary: The peer is currently connected and ready to communicate with.

constants.name: STATE_DISCONNECT_LATER
constants.summary: The peer is expected to disconnect after it has no more outgoing packets to send.

constants.name: STATE_DISCONNECTING
constants.summary: The peer is currently disconnecting.

constants.name: STATE_ACKNOWLEDGING_DISCONNECT
constants.summary: The peer has acknowledged the disconnection request.

constants.name: STATE_ZOMBIE
constants.summary: The peer has lost connection, but is not considered truly disconnected (as the peer didn't acknowledge the disconnection request).

constants.name: PEER_PACKET_LOSS
constants.summary: Mean packet loss of reliable packets as a ratio with respect to the [constant PACKET_LOSS_SCALE].

constants.name: PEER_PACKET_LOSS_VARIANCE
constants.summary: Packet loss variance.

constants.name: PEER_PACKET_LOSS_EPOCH
constants.summary: The time at which packet loss statistics were last updated (in milliseconds since the connection started). The interval for packet loss statistics updates is 10 seconds, and at least one packet must have been sent since the last statistics update.

constants.name: PEER_ROUND_TRIP_TIME
constants.summary: Mean packet round trip time for reliable packets.

constants.name: PEER_ROUND_TRIP_TIME_VARIANCE
constants.summary: Variance of the mean round trip time.

constants.name: PEER_LAST_ROUND_TRIP_TIME
constants.summary: Last recorded round trip time for a reliable packet.

constants.name: PEER_LAST_ROUND_TRIP_TIME_VARIANCE
constants.summary: Variance of the last trip time recorded.

constants.name: PEER_PACKET_THROTTLE
constants.summary: The peer's current throttle status.

constants.name: PEER_PACKET_THROTTLE_LIMIT
constants.summary: The maximum number of unreliable packets that should not be dropped. This value is always greater than or equal to [code]1[/code]. The initial value is equal to [constant PACKET_THROTTLE_SCALE].

constants.name: PEER_PACKET_THROTTLE_COUNTER
constants.summary: Internal value used to increment the packet throttle counter. The value is hardcoded to [code]7[/code] and cannot be changed. You probably want to look at [constant PEER_PACKET_THROTTLE_ACCELERATION] instead.

constants.name: PEER_PACKET_THROTTLE_EPOCH
constants.summary: The time at which throttle statistics were last updated (in milliseconds since the connection started). The interval for throttle statistics updates is [constant PEER_PACKET_THROTTLE_INTERVAL].

constants.name: PEER_PACKET_THROTTLE_ACCELERATION
constants.summary: The throttle's acceleration factor. Higher values will make ENet adapt to fluctuating network conditions faster, causing unrelaible packets to be sent [i]more[/i] often. The default value is [code]2[/code].

constants.name: PEER_PACKET_THROTTLE_DECELERATION
constants.summary: The throttle's deceleration factor. Higher values will make ENet adapt to fluctuating network conditions faster, causing unrelaible packets to be sent [i]less[/i] often. The default value is [code]2[/code].

constants.name: PEER_PACKET_THROTTLE_INTERVAL
constants.summary: The interval over which the lowest mean round trip time should be measured for use by the throttle mechanism (in milliseconds). The default value is [code]5000[/code].

constants.name: PACKET_LOSS_SCALE
constants.summary: The reference scale for packet loss. See [method get_statistic] and [constant PEER_PACKET_LOSS].

constants.name: PACKET_THROTTLE_SCALE
constants.summary: The reference value for throttle configuration. The default value is [code]32[/code]. See [method throttle_configure].

constants.name: FLAG_RELIABLE
constants.summary: Mark the packet to be sent as reliable.

constants.name: FLAG_UNSEQUENCED
constants.summary: Mark the packet to be sent unsequenced (unreliable).

constants.name: FLAG_UNRELIABLE_FRAGMENT
constants.summary: Mark the packet to be sent unreliable even if the packet is too big and needs fragmentation (increasing the chance of it being dropped).
